tests.title: All vertex shaders declare vDepth varying
tests.description: Each of the four vertex shaders (pointWarp, particleWarp, crystalWarp, ribbonWarp) must declare a `varying float vDepth` to pass camera-space depth to the fragment stage.
steps: Read all four vertex shader sources from src/visual/shaders/
Parse varying declarations using parseGlslVaryings()
Assert each shader declares a varying named 'vDepth' of type 'float'
tests.expected: All four vertex shaders contain `varying float vDepth` declaration
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: All fragment shaders declare vDepth varying
tests.description: Each fragment shader must receive the vDepth varying from the vertex stage to compute fog attenuation.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources from src/visual/shaders/
Parse varying declarations using parseGlslVaryings()
Assert each shader declares a varying named 'vDepth' of type 'float'
tests.expected: All four fragment shaders contain `varying float vDepth` declaration
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: Vertex shaders assign vDepth from camera-space depth
tests.description: Each vertex shader must assign vDepth from the existing depth calculation (derived from mvPosition.z).
steps: Read all four vertex shader sources
Search for assignment pattern matching `vDepth = depth` or `vDepth = ` followed by a depth-related expression
Assert each shader contains the vDepth assignment
tests.expected: All four vertex shaders contain `vDepth = depth;` or equivalent assignment
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: vDepth varying is consistent between vertex and fragment shaders
tests.description: The vDepth varying must have matching type declarations in both vertex and fragment shaders for each geometry system.
steps: Parse varyings from each vertex/fragment shader pair
Assert vDepth exists in both vertex and fragment shaders
Assert the type matches (float) in both
tests.expected: vDepth varying type matches between vertex and fragment shaders for all four systems
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: All fragment shaders declare uFogNear and uFogFar uniforms
tests.description: Fragment shaders need fog range uniforms to compute depth-based attenuation.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources
Parse uniform declarations using parseGlslUniforms()
Assert each shader declares `uniform float uFogNear` and `uniform float uFogFar`
tests.expected: All four fragment shaders contain uFogNear and uFogFar uniform declarations
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: uFogNear and uFogFar are registered in COMMON_UNIFORMS
tests.description: New fog uniforms must be registered in the shader registry with correct types and sensible defaults.
steps: Import COMMON_UNIFORMS from shaderRegistry
Find entries named 'uFogNear' and 'uFogFar'
Assert both exist with type 'float'
Assert both have numeric default values
Assert uFogNear default < uFogFar default (near must be closer than far)
tests.expected: COMMON_UNIFORMS includes uFogNear and uFogFar with float type and valid defaults where near < far
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: Fragment shaders compute fog factor using smoothstep
tests.description: Fog attenuation must use smoothstep for continuous, non-hard-edge depth transition.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources
Search for smoothstep usage involving uFogNear, uFogFar, and vDepth
Assert each shader contains a smoothstep-based fog factor computation
tests.expected: All four fragment shaders contain `smoothstep(uFogNear, uFogFar, vDepth)` or equivalent
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation
Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: Fragment shaders apply fog factor to alpha (not opaque fog color)
tests.description: Fog must reduce alpha rather than mix toward a solid fog color, preserving additive blending aesthetic.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources
Verify the fog factor is applied as an alpha multiplier (e.g., `(1.0 - fogFactor * ...)` in the alpha computation)
Verify no solid fog color (vec3) is mixed into the rgb channels as a replacement
tests.expected: Fog factor modulates alpha channel; no solid-color fog replacement in rgb
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation
Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: Fog alpha attenuation is capped below 100%
tests.description: Far points must not fully vanish — the fog factor should be capped so distant geometry becomes ghostly, not invisible.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources
Find the fog-to-alpha application line
Assert the fog factor is multiplied by a cap value less than 1.0 (e.g., 0.85)
Verify far points retain nonzero alpha
tests.expected: Fog alpha multiplier is capped (e.g., `fogFactor * 0.85`) so maximum attenuation < 100%
acceptanceCriteria: Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: Fragment shaders apply depth-based color desaturation
tests.description: Far geometry should appear visually distinct via desaturation/cool-shift, not just alpha fade.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources
Search for luminance computation (dot product with luma coefficients)
Search for mix() or lerp between original color and a desaturated/cool-shifted variant using fogFactor
Assert both patterns are present
tests.expected: All four fragment shaders compute luminance and mix toward a desaturated color based on depth
acceptanceCriteria: Far geometry appears visually distinct from near geometry

tests.title: Color desaturation is subtle (factor < 1.0)
tests.description: The desaturation must be partial, not total, to remain atmospheric rather than game-like.
steps: Read all four fragment shader sources
Find the desaturation mix factor (e.g., `fogFactor * 0.5`)
Assert the factor is less than 1.0
tests.expected: Desaturation mix factor multiplied by a value ≤ 0.5, keeping the effect subtle
acceptanceCriteria: Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like
Far geometry appears visually distinct from near geometry

tests.title: Vertex shaders include atmospheric decay on point size
tests.description: Far points should shrink faster than pure perspective to reinforce atmospheric depth.
steps: Read all four vertex shader sources
Search for an exponential decay term (exp(-...) or similar) applied to point size
Assert the decay references uFogNear as the start threshold
Assert the decay term is multiplied into the pointSize calculation
tests.expected: All four vertex shaders contain atmospheric decay (exp-based) in pointSize computation
acceptanceCriteria: Point size or brightness varies with distance

tests.title: Atmospheric decay rate is conservative
tests.description: The decay rate constant should be small enough that far points don't vanish on low-DPR displays.
steps: Read all four vertex shader sources
Extract the exponential decay rate constant from the atmospheric decay expression
Assert the rate is ≤ 0.15 (conservative range)
tests.expected: Atmospheric decay rate constant is ≤ 0.15
acceptanceCriteria: Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: uFogNear uniform is declared in all vertex shaders for atmospheric decay
tests.description: The vertex shader atmospheric decay references uFogNear, so it must be declared there too.
steps: Read all four vertex shader sources
Parse uniform declarations
Assert uFogNear is declared as a float uniform in each vertex shader
tests.expected: All four vertex shaders declare `uniform float uFogNear`
acceptanceCriteria: Point size or brightness varies with distance

tests.title: Existing gl_PointSize clamp still present
tests.description: The existing minimum point size clamp (2.5) must remain to prevent sub-pixel points on any display.
steps: Read all four vertex shader sources
Search for gl_PointSize clamp with minimum value ≥ 2.0
Assert the clamp is present in each shader
tests.expected: All four vertex shaders contain `clamp(pointSize, 2.5, ...)` or equivalent minimum
acceptanceCriteria: Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: System TypeScript files include uFogNear and uFogFar in ShaderMaterial uniforms
tests.description: Each geometry system must pass the fog uniforms to its ShaderMaterial.
steps: Read pointCloud.ts, particleField.ts, crystalField.ts, ribbonField.ts
Search for uFogNear and uFogFar in the uniforms object of each ShaderMaterial
Assert both uniforms are present in all four system files
tests.expected: All four system files include uFogNear and uFogFar in their ShaderMaterial uniforms
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: All new GLSL uniforms pass shader integrity checks
tests.description: New fog uniforms must pass the existing T-049-03 integrity check — every GLSL uniform must be in COMMON_UNIFORMS.
steps: Import parseGlslUniforms and COMMON_UNIFORMS
Parse uniforms from all vertex and fragment shaders
Build registry map from COMMON_UNIFORMS
Assert every parsed uniform (including uFogNear, uFogFar) exists in registry with correct type
tests.expected: No uniform declaration exists in GLSL that is missing from COMMON_UNIFORMS
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: No undeclared identifiers after depth cue additions
tests.description: All new GLSL identifiers (vDepth, fogFactor, uFogNear, uFogFar, lum, fogTint, atmosphericDecay) must be properly declared.
steps: Use the same identifier extraction approach as T-049-04
Build allowlist from builtins, declared uniforms/attributes/varyings, local variables, and function names
Assert no undeclared identifiers exist in any vertex or fragment shader
tests.expected: Zero undeclared identifiers across all eight shader files
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: Fog uniforms have consistent declarations across all fragment shaders
tests.description: All four fragment shaders should declare identical fog-related uniforms to ensure consistent depth cue behavior.
steps: Parse uniforms from all four fragment shaders
Extract fog-related uniforms (uFogNear, uFogFar)
Assert all four fragment shaders declare the same fog uniforms with matching types
tests.expected: uFogNear and uFogFar are identically declared in all four fragment shaders
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: smoothstep fog produces 0.0 at near distance and 1.0 at far distance
tests.description: Verify the mathematical correctness of the smoothstep fog model: no fog at near, full fog at far.
steps: Implement GLSL smoothstep in JS: smoothstep(edge0, edge1, x)
Compute fogFactor at depth = uFogNear → expect 0.0
Compute fogFactor at depth = uFogFar → expect 1.0
Compute fogFactor at depth = midpoint → expect ~0.5
Compute fogFactor at depth < uFogNear → expect 0.0 (no fog for near geometry)
tests.expected: smoothstep yields 0.0 at near, 1.0 at far, monotonically increasing between
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation
Far geometry appears visually distinct from near geometry

tests.title: Fog alpha cap ensures far points remain visible
tests.description: With the 0.85 cap, even at maximum fog factor, alpha should remain > 0.
steps: Compute effective alpha at max fog: baseAlpha * (1.0 - 1.0 * 0.85) = baseAlpha * 0.15
Assert this value is > 0 for any positive baseAlpha
Verify at zero fog (near): alpha is unchanged (baseAlpha * 1.0)
tests.expected: Minimum alpha at maximum fog is 15% of base alpha (nonzero, ghostly)
acceptanceCriteria: Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: Atmospheric point size decay at near distance equals 1.0
tests.description: Points nearer than uFogNear should not be affected by atmospheric decay.
steps: Compute atmosphericDecay = exp(-rate * max(depth - fogNear, 0.0)) for depth < fogNear
Assert result is exp(0) = 1.0
Compute for depth = fogNear, assert result is 1.0
Compute for depth >> fogNear, assert result < 1.0 but > 0.0
tests.expected: Decay is 1.0 at and below fogNear, smoothly decreasing beyond
acceptanceCriteria: Point size or brightness varies with distance

tests.title: Production build succeeds with depth cue changes
tests.description: npm run build must pass without TypeScript errors or shader-related build failures.
steps: Run npm run build
Assert exit code is 0
Assert no TypeScript compilation errors
tests.expected: Clean production build with zero errors
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation
Point size or brightness varies with distance
Far geometry appears visually distinct from near geometry
Depth effect remains subtle and non-game-like

tests.title: No forbidden storage APIs in modified files
tests.description: Privacy constraint: none of the modified shader or system files may use localStorage, cookies, etc.
steps: Read all modified TypeScript system files
Read all modified GLSL shader files
Assert none contain localStorage, sessionStorage, document.cookie, indexedDB, or openDatabase
tests.expected: Zero forbidden storage API references in any modified file
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation

tests.title: Depth cue fog effect is consistent across all four geometry modes
tests.description: All four fragment shaders must implement the same fog algorithm so depth cues look consistent regardless of active geometry mode.
steps: Extract the fog-related code block from each fragment shader (lines involving fogFactor, lum, fogTint, alpha modification)
Compare the fog logic across all four shaders
Assert the fog computation pattern is identical (smoothstep, alpha cap, desaturation factor)
tests.expected: All four fragment shaders use the same fog algorithm with matching constants
acceptanceCriteria: Scene includes fog or depth-based attenuation
Far geometry appears visually distinct from near geometry
